**Day 2 Mouchette Impressions** (Aggregated from current top EN players)

**Is Mouchette Powerful:** She is an ok, A~B tier dps. She can clear content. She is not top tier or overpowered. She won't break records unless you invest heavily into her and whale for her and her wheel

**Stopping Point for Pulls:** E3, but OE is good. I don't recommend using her before E3

**Best Teams:** Mouchette, Helot, Thais, Aigis/other supports. High investment accounts can also try mono chaos with Mouchette, Hameln, Ramona, Ryker/Alva

**Can I Use Her With Lotan/Gelot:** I don't recommend it as you will effectively have 2 dps on the team. A support that adds a damage multiplier to Mouchette, or adds defense or utility which Mouchette lacks, will be more effective than another dps

**Covenant:** Dreams of Medicine (recommended) or Burial Grounds' Sighs (if you have a good set). Prioritize Crit, Death Resist, Keyflare Regen

**Wheels:** Crit, Death Resist, Strike wheels work best. Best in slot are  her own wheel + Lily wheel. Lotan wheel, Blade of the Titan, etc. are fine

**Should I Pull Mouchette:** If you like her.

**Should I Pull Mouchette Wheel:** If you are prepared to invest heavily in her. There are other wheels that can substitute

Maybe not entirely teambuilding related, but I couldn't find info anywhere. How am I supposed prioritize upgrading skills and such? I'm mostly just upgrading the first few cheap levels on the units I use a lot, but is there some prio to that?

it depends on the unit

first you should get everyone to skill level 3 because it doesnt use timegated materials and is basically free
basically, upgrading skills only makes the numbers of that skill bigger
so there are some that are low impact, and some that are high impact
for example, if you never rouse a support
theres no point upgrading their rouse
if you always sell a card
also thais is like the ultimate support
dont upgrade it
conversely if its your carry's main damage card
you want to get it as high as possible
it should be pretty obvious if you think about it
and finally dont neglect strike and defense because they give aliemus
Right yeah, that makes sense
there are also skills where upgrading it doesnt actually make the skill better
like the numbers on horla cards barely matter
you use them for the passive mood buff, not because you want to deal damage with horla strike or whatever
so horla cards are low priority
Same goes for the exhalt upgrade I imagine? Just more expensive resources
yea there are exalts that are super worth upgrading, and there are ones that you can leave at level 2
Tawil exalt off the top of my head gives you 20 more keyflare per skill level
which is tremendously mid
you activate it to get cards and copy her next skill, not to get 20 more keyflare
upgrading corpo exalt gives you a bigger shield lol
Still good but there are higher priority upgrades
Understood
oh, but remember that the cards she pulls are the level of her exalt!
it what
ok i didnt know this
it really should say that in the skill description...

It's awful ;-;, when you poof a card 
out of nothing, the card level is dictated by the level of the card that generated it
i knew that was true for pinions of time but not for exalt

Her gland division gens another gland division

The lvl 1 power of friendship will generate lvl 1 gland division

Blue 24 is the worst form of 24 
Ultra 24 is a S tiers character just by existing
Chaos 24 offers things MonoChaos lacks and needs while providing things MonoChaos wants
Caro 24 is so good it can carry even Doresain in A5
Only Blue 24 is a way to ruin an account since she wants supports that can carry entire teams on their own while providing almost nothing of value herself that can't be find elsewhere
Are colors 24 locked in? Meaning if you have ultra 24 can you switch to Caro 24 in the next exploration/fight or your account now has ultra 24 and that's it?

24 colors depend on the team 
But no she isn't locked
If you put 24  in a caro team, she is caro, if you put her in monochaos she is chaos, etc
The reason why I say Blue 24 bricks an account is that Blue 24 is a whale character that wants whaled out supports 
So if you play Blue 24 in endgame you are only using 1 team instead of up to 4 teams 
Besides Blue 24 main gimmick is poison and blue is a poor choice of realm for the status 
Miryam and Faros are the only 2 who synergize with it, and they are both Lemurian so they are much better in a dedicated Lemurian team with Gmurphy that can clear the same content with a much lower investment level

All the effects that give both crit rate and crit damage simultaneously
More often that not they give the same amount of both
and also
crit rate will hit a cap if you have enough buffs

cause i have paradox + tinct exalt + horla exalt
crit dmg is unbounded
now obviously the best for every comp is to look at all the sources of crit rate and crit dmg
sit down and theorycraft the best proportion
but as a GENERAL RULE
i think its safer to build crit dmg on covenants for example

I mean
It's more an issue of temp crit rate being a bit ... undesirable
Unless you only do damage when you have 100%+ crit rate
If you have a cov where the effect gives 30% crit rate and crit dmg conditionally
lets say paradox
what is the average damage if you build crit rate vs crit dmg
if you build 20% more crit rate you are critting 50% of the time for 1.3x final dmg
so a 15% dps increase from crits
you get twice as much crit dmg from covenants and wheels
so you go and build 40% more crit dmg
now you are critting 30% of the time for 1.7x final dmg
thats a 35% dps increase
im sorry
i forgot to add base crit
but you get my point

There a number of issues with your maths
First : 
- You don't get twice as much crit damage compared to crit rate in Morimens
Second 
- Every character in the game starts with at least 50% crit damage 
So minimum damage for a crit is 1.5x damage

Third
Paradox is 20% crit rate and damage <:cute1:1356053053568192717>

the ratio in Morimens is 2 points of crit rate for every 3 points of crit damage

At level 45+ you unlock substats for covenants (the infinite reroll equipment to grind for in this game)
It means that the ratio of crit rate vs crit damage for covenant substats is 1% crit rate or 1.5% crit damage
